svg.font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
svg.text: Where does the AGENT actually come in?
Walk through qid 28 from question to validated record · LEFT = agent reasoning · RIGHT = server determinism · ★ = real judgment moment · 🛡 = discipline-enforcing guard

g.text: AGENT — LLM thinking + tool selection
SERVER — MCP tool body, deterministic
JUDGMENT — non-procedural decision the LLM owns
GUARD — server pushes back on shortcuts
INPUT · POST /question/california_schools/28
"Consider the average difference between K-12 and 15-17 enrollment of locally-funded schools, list names + DOC of schools above this average."
AGENT EQUIPMENT · what the LLM is loaded with at startup — does NOT change per question
SYSTEM PROMPT · apps/bird_invocable_api_creator/main.py:_SYSTEM
teaches a methodology — applied by the LLM, not enforced by code
★ Decomposition: subquery in WHERE / CTE / "and then" → ≥2 tools
★ Naming: concept-led, domain-meaningful — no question_*, *_solver, gold_*
★ Slot lifting: lift literals to params · examples ≠ the question's answer
★ Reuse first: tool_list before tool_register; reuse w/ different args
closed loop: register → tool_call → seq_record → seq_validate (retry × ≤2)
TOOL SURFACE · load_tools(["invocable_apis"]) · 18 MCP tools, 5 families
all on mcp-invocable_apis (port 29107) · same envelope contract
db_get_schema
db_sample_rows
db_run_sql
bird_* · 4
bird_list_databases
bird_list_questions
bird_get_question
bird_run_gold
tool_* · 5
tool_register
tool_list
tool_get
tool_call
tool_delete
seq_* · 3
seq_record
seq_execute
seq_validate
dataset_* · ignore_* · 3
dataset_emit · ignore_set · ignore_list
AGENT · CugaAgent inside FastAPI app
SERVER · mcp-invocable_apis · port 29107
PHASE 1 · ORIENT · agent decides what it needs to know
Agent reasons (procedural — driven by the prompt)
"I need: the question + evidence + gold SQL → bird_get_question."
"I need an oracle → bird_run_gold."
"I haven't seen the schema yet → db_get_schema. Then sample_rows."
"FundingType looked free-text — I should DISTINCT it before designing."
7 read-only calls; the order is procedural — but the count varies by question.
Server executes 7 read-only tools
bird_get_question · bird_run_gold (runs gold SQL on real SQLite)
db_get_schema · tool_list · db_sample_rows × 2
→ Returns canonical 57 rows, DDL, value vocabularies. Read-only;
no state mutated. Connection opened with mode=ro.
PHASE 2 · DECOMPOSE · ★ THE KEY JUDGMENT — agent reads gold SQL and decides "1 tool or N?"
★ Agent JUDGMENT · no tool call — pure reasoning over what it just read
Observed: Gold SQL has a subquery in WHERE — strongest decomposition signal in the system prompt.
Decided: 2 tools. Tool A = the AVG (subquery). Tool B = the outer filter, takes threshold + funding_type.
Why it's judgment, not pattern: the prompt teaches signals; the agent has to apply them and pick a boundary.
A one-shot LLM script paste-translates the SQL — never decomposes. Reality: only 1 of 67 passing did this.
PHASE 3 · DESIGN + REGISTER TOOL 1 · ★ slot lifting + naming · 🛡 server fires 4 guards
Agent designs the tool — multiple judgments stacked
Naming: "avg_enrollment_difference_by_funding_type" (concept-led, not "question_28_solver")
Slot lift: "Locally funded" → parameter funding_type
Examples: ["Directly funded", ...] — NOT "Locally funded" (would leak the answer)
Code: uses backtick-quoted column names confirmed in Phase 1
Then calls tool_register(...) — purely procedural after the design is fixed.
Server pushes back BEFORE persisting — 4 guards
Forbidden-name pattern: regex rejects question_*, *_solver, gold_*, todo_*, *_wrapper
SQL skeleton dup: strip literals — same skeleton as existing tool? → reject + name it
Name-token Jaccard ≥ 0.85: cosmetic dup like *_v2 → reject
_compile_tool sandbox: exec into {sqlite3, json, re} only — no other imports
All pass → INSERT into state/california_schools.db / table tools. Otherwise: agent re-tries with the message as feedback.
PHASE 4 · SMOKE-TEST · prompt requires immediate tool_call after every register — fail-fast on bugs
Agent reasons (procedural)
"Prompt says: every tool_register is followed by an immediate tool_call."
Calls tool_call(name=..., args={"funding_type": "Locally funded"})
Server executes generated code
opens mode=ro SQLite connection · runs the registered run(conn, **kwargs)
→ {output: {"avg_difference": 16.7…}} · any code bug surfaces HERE, not at eval
↻ Repeat Phases 3–4 for Tool 2 · agent designs schools_with_enrollment_difference_above_funding(funding_type, threshold) · guards pass · smoke-test returns 57 rows
PHASE 5 · COMPOSE SEQUENCE · ★ agent designs cross-step variable binding
★ Agent JUDGMENT · how do these tools chain?
"Tool A's avg_difference output → Tool B's threshold input"
Designs: step 1 with bind: "avg"; step 2 args use "{{avg.avg_difference}}"
Calls seq_record(qid=28, sequence=[...])
Server persists the sequence
Stores in sequences table keyed by question_id.
No execution yet — record is just the plan. Validation is the next step.
→ {recorded: true}
PHASE 6 · VALIDATE · 🛡 server runs the closed-loop oracle — agent only reads outcome
Agent reasons (procedural)
"Prompt requires seq_validate after every seq_record."
Calls seq_validate(qid=28) · reads response · passed=true → done; false → fix
Server runs the oracle — entirely deterministic
execute gold SQL · _run_sequence with {{var}} resolution · _normalize_for_compare
set-equality compare (floats round 6dp · sort-within-row · sort-row-list)
→ {passed: true, ...} · persisted to validations table · the agent does not own this verdict
FINAL OUTPUT · output/california_schools/dataset.json[28]
{question_id: 28, gold_sql, gold_result, tool_sequence: [...], final_result: [...], validated: true, failure_msg: null} · tools.py +2 · validation_report++

svg.text: Real agent value lives in the ★ moments: decomposition, naming, slot lifting, sequence binding, retry on failure. Everything else is procedural.
